params.name: Kinkaku-ji Model
params.description: Kinkaku-ji, also known as the Golden Pavilion, is a famous Buddhist temple located in Kyoto, Japan, renowned for its golden exterior. The temple was built by Ashikaga Yoshimitsu, the third shogun of the Muromachi shogunate, as his villa, later converted into a Zen Buddhist temple. The most notable feature of Kinkaku-ji is that its second and third floors are completely covered in gold leaf, making the entire structure shine brilliantly in the sunlight, reflecting in the pond, creating a spectacu …
